finding.summary: Stereo image is strongly decorrelated: L/R correlation is -0.42 and the side channel sits 12 dB above the mid (-15.0 dB vs -27.0 dB) while balance is essentially centered (0.2 dB). For a pad this implies heavy widening that will cancel substantially when summed to mono. Headroom is fine (true peak -5.5 dB, sample peak -6.0 dB, crest factor 12.0 dB, integrated LUFS -20.0, LRA 8.0 LU) and silence_fraction is 0.05, so level statistics are trustworthy.
finding.probable_cause: The signal entering the track carries significant anti-correlated stereo content (likely intentional stereo-widening or layered, decorrelated oscillator/detune sources) producing a side-dominant field without any post-processing in the FX chain to manage mono compatibility or width.
finding.confidence_reason: Negative correlation of -0.42 and side-to-mid ratio of +12 dB are directly measured and consistent; medium (not high) because the track is named 'Wide Pad' so the widening may be intentional, and without source/bus context we cannot judge whether mono compatibility matters for this arrangement.

evidence_refs.description: L/R correlation -0.42 indicates substantial anti-phase content
Side level -15.0 dB, 12 dB above mid
Centered at 0.2 dB, so the decorrelation is from width, not panning
12.0 dB, healthy dynamics for a pad

proposal.reason: The decorrelated stereo field is the only material issue and it originates in the source content, not in track-level controls. fx_chain is empty, so there is no widener/MS/decorrelation processor available to bypass or adjust; track volume (0.0 dB) and pan (0.0) are already neutral. Making a safe, verified single move requires an FX target that does not exist in this payload, so no operation is proposed.
